tables.query:
WITH moments AS (
  SELECT
    lead_bucket_min,
    SUM(n_observations) AS n_observations,
    COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date) AS n_snapshots,
    SUM(sum_fare) AS sum_fare,
    SUM(sum_sq_fare) AS sum_sq_fare,
    MIN(min_fare) AS min_fare,
    MAX(max_fare) AS max_fare,
    SUM(sum_ln_fare) AS sum_ln_fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min
),

-- Independent-sample count: distinct anchors, NOT additively recoverable from the
-- summed stats, so computed straight from the small trip grain.
anchors AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
    WHEN days_to_departure < 14 THEN NULL
    WHEN days_to_departure < 21 THEN 14
    WHEN days_to_departure < 30 THEN 21
    WHEN days_to_departure < 45 THEN 30
    WHEN days_to_departure < 60 THEN 45
    WHEN days_to_departure < 90 THEN 60
    WHEN days_to_departure < 120 THEN 90
    WHEN days_to_departure < 180 THEN 120
    ELSE 180
  END AS lead_bucket_min,
    COUNT(DISTINCT outbound_date) AS n_distinct_anchors
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE stay_length_days = 7
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min
)

SELECT
  m.lead_bucket_min,
  CASE m.lead_bucket_min
    WHEN 14 THEN '14-20'
    WHEN 21 THEN '21-29'
    WHEN 30 THEN '30-44'
    WHEN 45 THEN '45-59'
    WHEN 60 THEN '60-89'
    WHEN 90 THEN '90-119'
    WHEN 120 THEN '120-179'
    WHEN 180 THEN '180+'
  END AS lead_time_bucket,
  m.n_observations,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) AS n_distinct_anchors,
  m.n_snapshots,
  SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations) AS mean_fare,
  IF(
    m.n_observations >= 2,
    SQRT(
      GREATEST(0.0, SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_sq_fare, m.n_observations)
                    - POW(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations), 2))
      * m.n_observations / (m.n_observations - 1)
    ),
    NULL
  ) AS stddev_fare,
  EXP(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_ln_fare, m.n_observations)) AS geomean_fare,
  ROUND(m.min_fare, 2) AS min_fare,
  ROUND(m.max_fare, 2) AS max_fare,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) >= 5 AS is_reliable
FROM moments m
LEFT JOIN anchors a USING (lead_bucket_min)
ORDER BY m.lead_bucket_min

actionDescriptor.description: The booking curve: expected CHEAPEST fare vs lead time (grain: one row per lead_time_bucket) for the DEFAULT-STAY product. Marginal over departure_month — sums the additive sufficient stats of int_daily_fare_stats by lead bucket (no history rescan). MATURITY: is_reliable gates on DISTINCT ANCHORS (independent trips), not n_observations (the panel re-observes each anchor, inflating observation count). stddev_fare is NULL when n_observations < 2. This marginal is for reporting/EDA and as a sparse-cell fallback; it is NOT the residual model's primary predictor — the panel makes lead & month collinear, so the honest expected surface is the joint (lead × month) cell in the residual model. Per-segment (hub/carrier/stay) baselines are DEFERRED, not dropped.

columns.description: Integer lower bound of the lead-time bucket (join key; same includes/lead_time logic as observations).
Human-readable lead-time range, e.g. '60-89'.
Trip-OBSERVATIONS pooled into this bucket (inflated by panel re-observation — not an independent-sample count).
Distinct departure dates (independent trips) in this bucket. The honest sample size; drives is_reliable.
Expected (mean) cheapest fare for this lead-time bucket.
Sample standard deviation (n-1). NULL when n_observations < 2.
n_distinct_anchors >= threshold — conservative gate for trusting this bucket's expectation.

tables.query:
WITH moments AS (
  SELECT
    lead_bucket_min,
    departure_month,
    SUM(n_observations) AS n_observations,
    COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date) AS n_snapshots,
    SUM(sum_fare) AS sum_fare,
    SUM(sum_sq_fare) AS sum_sq_fare,
    MIN(min_fare) AS min_fare,
    MAX(max_fare) AS max_fare,
    SUM(sum_ln_fare) AS sum_ln_fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min, departure_month
),

-- Independent-sample count: distinct anchors, NOT additively recoverable from the
-- summed stats, so computed straight from the small trip grain (same gate the
-- residual model and both marginal baselines use).
anchors AS (
  SELECT
    lead_bucket_min,
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM outbound_date) AS departure_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT outbound_date) AS n_distinct_anchors
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL
    AND stay_length_days = 7
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min, departure_month
)

SELECT
  m.lead_bucket_min,
  CASE m.lead_bucket_min
    WHEN 14 THEN '14-20'
    WHEN 21 THEN '21-29'
    WHEN 30 THEN '30-44'
    WHEN 45 THEN '45-59'
    WHEN 60 THEN '60-89'
    WHEN 90 THEN '90-119'
    WHEN 120 THEN '120-179'
    WHEN 180 THEN '180+'
  END AS lead_time_bucket,
  m.departure_month,
  CASE m.departure_month
    WHEN 1 THEN 'Jan' WHEN 2 THEN 'Feb' WHEN 3 THEN 'Mar' WHEN 4 THEN 'Apr'
    WHEN 5 THEN 'May' WHEN 6 THEN 'Jun' WHEN 7 THEN 'Jul' WHEN 8 THEN 'Aug'
    WHEN 9 THEN 'Sep' WHEN 10 THEN 'Oct' WHEN 11 THEN 'Nov' WHEN 12 THEN 'Dec'
  END AS month_name,
  m.n_observations,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) AS n_distinct_anchors,
  m.n_snapshots,
  SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations) AS mean_fare,
  IF(
    m.n_observations >= 2,
    SQRT(
      GREATEST(0.0, SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_sq_fare, m.n_observations)
                    - POW(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations), 2))
      * m.n_observations / (m.n_observations - 1)
    ),
    NULL
  ) AS stddev_fare,
  EXP(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_ln_fare, m.n_observations)) AS geomean_fare,
  ROUND(m.min_fare, 2) AS min_fare,
  ROUND(m.max_fare, 2) AS max_fare,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) >= 5 AS is_reliable
FROM moments m
LEFT JOIN anchors a USING (lead_bucket_min, departure_month)
ORDER BY m.lead_bucket_min, m.departure_month

actionDescriptor.description: The JOINT expectation surface: expected CHEAPEST fare per (lead_time_bucket × departure_month) cell (grain: one row per joint cell) for the DEFAULT-STAY product. This is the honest expected surface the residual model scores against — booking_curve_baseline and seasonal_baseline are its two marginals (summed over month / over lead), useful for EDA but BIASED as a predictor because the panel makes lead & month collinear (a mid-July snapshot forces Aug=short-lead, spring=long-lead). Exposing the joint cell lets Looker render the booking-curve HEATMAP (lead × month → mean fare) directly instead of reconstructing it from marginals. Sums the additive sufficient stats of int_daily_fare_stats by (lead_bucket_min, departure_month) — no history rescan; the LOO per-observation variant lives in fct_fare_anomaly. MATURITY: is_reliable gates on DISTINCT ANCHORS (independent trips), not n_observations (the panel re-observes each anchor). Today the surface is DIAGONAL and sparse — that collinearity is exactly why the joint model exists, so the sparse diagonal is the visual argument, not a defect. stddev_fare NULL when n_observations < 2.

columns.description: Integer lower bound of the lead-time bucket (heatmap X join key; same includes/lead_time logic as observations).
Human-readable lead-time range, e.g. '60-89'.
Month of the departure date (1-12), month-of-year so it reads as seasonality (heatmap Y).
Human-readable month.
Trip-OBSERVATIONS pooled into this cell (inflated by panel re-observation — not an independent-sample count).
Distinct departure dates (independent trips) in this cell. The honest sample size; drives is_reliable.
Expected (mean) cheapest fare for this joint cell — the heatmap color metric.
Sample standard deviation (n-1). NULL when n_observations < 2.
Geometric mean cheapest fare (log-space center; fares are heavy-tailed).
n_distinct_anchors >= threshold — conservative gate for trusting this cell's expectation.

tables.query:
WITH trips AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date,
    lead_bucket_min,
    departure_month,
    CAST(cheapest_fare AS FLOAT64) AS fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL             -- drop out-of-panel leads (< 14 days)
    AND stay_length_days = 7
  
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  lead_bucket_min,
  departure_month,
  COUNT(*) AS n_observations,
  SUM(fare) AS sum_fare,
  SUM(POW(fare, 2)) AS sum_sq_fare,
  MIN(fare) AS min_fare,
  MAX(fare) AS max_fare,
  SUM(LN(fare)) AS sum_ln_fare,
  SUM(POW(LN(fare), 2)) AS sum_sq_ln_fare
FROM trips
GROUP BY snapshot_date, lead_bucket_min, departure_month

actionDescriptor.description: Additive sufficient statistics behind both marginal baselines (grain: snapshot_date × lead_time_bucket × departure_month), aggregated from int_trip_fares filtered to the DEFAULT STAY (product consistency — a 14/21-day trip must not enter a 7-day cell). Moments (n, sum, sum_sq, min/max, +log moments) are additive, so booking_curve_baseline sums over month and seasonal_baseline sums over lead-bucket, both exact. NOTE: n_observations counts trip-OBSERVATIONS, which the re-observing panel inflates beyond independent trips — the reliability gate downstream uses DISTINCT ANCHORS (computed separately from int_trip_fares), never this count. Incremental-correctness core: partitioned by snapshot_date + MERGE-replace on the cell key → boundary reprocessing is idempotent. Incremental by snapshot_date.

tables.incrementalQuery:
WITH trips AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date,
    lead_bucket_min,
    departure_month,
    CAST(cheapest_fare AS FLOAT64) AS fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL             -- drop out-of-panel leads (< 14 days)
    AND stay_length_days = 7
  AND snapshot_date >= (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`)
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  lead_bucket_min,
  departure_month,
  COUNT(*) AS n_observations,
  SUM(fare) AS sum_fare,
  SUM(POW(fare, 2)) AS sum_sq_fare,
  MIN(fare) AS min_fare,
  MAX(fare) AS max_fare,
  SUM(LN(fare)) AS sum_ln_fare,
  SUM(POW(LN(fare), 2)) AS sum_sq_ln_fare
FROM trips
GROUP BY snapshot_date, lead_bucket_min, departure_month

tables.query:
WITH anchors AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT outbound_date
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
),

point_events AS (
  SELECT event_id, event_category, start_date AS event_date, is_travel_peak
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
  WHERE event_shape = 'point'
),

windows AS (
  SELECT event_category, start_date, end_date
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
  WHERE event_shape = 'window'
),

-- Window membership: LOGICAL_OR over any overlapping window of each type. No
-- match → NULL → COALESCE'd to FALSE in the final select.
in_windows AS (
  SELECT
    a.outbound_date,
    LOGICAL_OR(w.event_category = 'ramadan') AS in_ramadan,
    LOGICAL_OR(w.event_category = 'summer_school_holiday') AS in_summer_school_holiday
  FROM anchors a
  LEFT JOIN windows w
    ON a.outbound_date BETWEEN w.start_date AND w.end_date
  GROUP BY a.outbound_date
),

-- Nearest Eid (the demand peaks): pick the min |distance|, tie-break by earlier date.
nearest_eid AS (
  SELECT
    a.outbound_date,
    p.event_category AS nearest_eid_category,
    p.event_date AS nearest_eid_date,
    DATE_DIFF(p.event_date, a.outbound_date, DAY) AS days_to_nearest_eid
  FROM anchors a
  CROSS JOIN point_events p
  WHERE p.event_category IN ('eid_al_fitr', 'eid_al_adha')
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    PARTITION BY a.outbound_date
    ORDER BY ABS(DATE_DIFF(p.event_date, a.outbound_date, DAY)), p.event_date
  ) = 1
),

-- Nearest lunar point event of any kind.
nearest_point AS (
  SELECT
    a.outbound_date,
    p.event_category AS nearest_point_event_category,
    p.is_travel_peak AS nearest_point_event_is_peak,
    DATE_DIFF(p.event_date, a.outbound_date, DAY) AS days_to_nearest_point_event
  FROM anchors a
  CROSS JOIN point_events p
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    PARTITION BY a.outbound_date
    ORDER BY ABS(DATE_DIFF(p.event_date, a.outbound_date, DAY)), p.event_date
  ) = 1
)

SELECT
  a.outbound_date,
  EXTRACT(MONTH FROM a.outbound_date) AS departure_month,
  COALESCE(iw.in_ramadan, FALSE) AS in_ramadan,
  COALESCE(iw.in_summer_school_holiday, FALSE) AS in_summer_school_holiday,
  e.days_to_nearest_eid,
  e.nearest_eid_category,
  e.nearest_eid_date,
  -- Travel-relevant zones around Eid (leisure travelers arrive shortly before to
  -- be there for the feast, then return). Coarse on purpose — sample is tiny.
  CASE
    WHEN e.days_to_nearest_eid BETWEEN -3 AND 10 THEN 'arrive_for_eid'
    WHEN e.days_to_nearest_eid BETWEEN 11 AND 30 THEN 'pre_eid_month'
    WHEN e.days_to_nearest_eid BETWEEN -30 AND -4 THEN 'post_eid'
    ELSE 'off_peak'
  END AS eid_proximity,
  np.days_to_nearest_point_event,
  np.nearest_point_event_category,
  np.nearest_point_event_is_peak
FROM anchors a
LEFT JOIN in_windows iw USING (outbound_date)
LEFT JOIN nearest_eid e USING (outbound_date)
LEFT JOIN nearest_point np USING (outbound_date)

actionDescriptor.description:
The event-enrichment bridge: maps each panel ANCHOR (outbound_date = when you travel) to its Moroccan-event context, so downstream layers can test whether fares/residuals move with proximity to Ramadan/Eid. Grain: one row per distinct outbound_date observed in int_trip_fares.

NON-INVASIVE BY DESIGN: a view over int_trip_fares × dim_events — the incremental spine (int_trip_fares / int_daily_fare_stats / fct_fare_anomaly) is untouched, so its verified idempotency is preserved. Nothing here feeds the z-score; this is the CORRELATION seam (docs/warehouse.md layer #5), not a new predictor.

TWO EVENT SHAPES, kept distinct (never conflated): WINDOWS (Ramadan, summer school) → in_* boolean flags; POINT events (Eid, Mawlid, Hijra) → SIGNED days-to-event. Sign convention: days_to_* = event_date − outbound_date, so POSITIVE = you depart BEFORE the event (event upcoming), NEGATIVE = you depart after it. The Eid columns single out the marquee diaspora demand peaks; the nearest_point_event_* columns generalize to any lunar point marker.

columns.description: Panel anchor / departure date the context is computed for.
EXTRACT(MONTH) — convenience join key back to the seasonal (month-of-year) baseline, for the fixed-vs-lunar comparison.
Departure falls inside a Ramadan window.
Departure falls inside the (approximate) Moroccan summer school-holiday window.
SIGNED days from departure to the nearest Eid (either al-Fitr or al-Adha). +ve = Eid upcoming after departure; -ve = departed after Eid. The marquee demand-proximity feature.
Which Eid the nearest-Eid distance refers to.
Date of that nearest Eid.
Coarse label bucketing days_to_nearest_eid into travel-relevant zones (arrive-for-Eid / pre-Eid month / post-Eid / off-peak).
SIGNED days to the nearest LUNAR point event of ANY kind (Eid/Mawlid/Hijra) — generalizes beyond Eid.
Category of that nearest point event.
Whether that nearest point event is a high-demand travel peak (an Eid) vs a minor marker.

tables.query:
WITH legs AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_offers`
  -- Reprocess the boundary partition (idempotent via MERGE + uniqueKey); scans
  -- only new/boundary partitions, never the whole table (constraint #2).
  
),

offers AS (
  SELECT
    -- Identity / grain
    snapshot_date,
    MAX(captured_at) AS captured_at,
    origin,
    destination,
    outbound_date,
    return_date,
    offer_list,
    offer_index,

    -- Derived trip axes (constraint #3: computed here, not at ingestion)
    DATE_DIFF(outbound_date, snapshot_date, DAY) AS days_to_departure,
    DATE_DIFF(return_date, outbound_date, DAY) AS stay_length_days,

    -- Price (round-trip total) + offer attributes
    MAX(offer_price) AS offer_price,
    ANY_VALUE(currency) AS currency,
    ANY_VALUE(offer_type) AS offer_type,
    MAX(total_duration_min) AS outbound_duration_min,
    MAX(carbon_emissions_g) AS carbon_emissions_g,
    ANY_VALUE(departure_token) AS departure_token,

    -- Routing microstructure (outbound)
    COUNT(*) AS n_legs,
    COUNT(*) - 1 AS stop_count,
    ARRAY_TO_STRING(
      ARRAY_CONCAT(
        [ARRAY_AGG(leg_departure_airport ORDER BY leg_index LIMIT 1)[OFFSET(0)]],
        ARRAY_AGG(leg_arrival_airport ORDER BY leg_index)
      ), '-'
    ) AS routing_path,
    IF(COUNT(*) > 1,
       ARRAY_AGG(leg_arrival_airport ORDER BY leg_index)[OFFSET(0)],
       NULL) AS primary_hub,

    -- Carriers / aircraft
    ARRAY_AGG(leg_airline ORDER BY leg_index LIMIT 1)[OFFSET(0)] AS primary_operating_carrier,
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT leg_airline, ', ') AS operating_carriers,
    ARRAY_AGG(
      SPLIT(leg_flight_number, ' ')[SAFE_OFFSET(0)] ORDER BY leg_index LIMIT 1
    )[OFFSET(0)] AS primary_marketing_carrier,
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT leg_airplane, ', ') AS aircraft_types,

    -- Timing microstructure
    ARRAY_AGG(leg_departure_time ORDER BY leg_index LIMIT 1)[OFFSET(0)] AS origin_departure_time,
    ARRAY_AGG(leg_arrival_time ORDER BY leg_index DESC LIMIT 1)[OFFSET(0)] AS destination_arrival_time,
    EXTRACT(DAYOFWEEK FROM outbound_date) AS outbound_dow
  FROM legs
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, origin, destination, outbound_date, return_date, offer_list, offer_index
)

SELECT
  *,
  EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) AS origin_departure_hour,
  -- NULL-safe: an unparseable departure time stays NULL rather than silently
  -- mislabeling as 'red_eye'.
  CASE
    WHEN origin_departure_time IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 5 AND 11 THEN 'morning'
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 12 AND 16 THEN 'afternoon'
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 17 AND 21 THEN 'evening'
    ELSE 'red_eye'
  END AS departure_time_bucket
FROM offers

actionDescriptor.description: One row per priced OFFER (grain: snapshot_date × outbound_date × return_date × offer_list × offer_index) — the analytical unit for booking-curve, dispersion, and residual-anomaly work. Rolls up the outbound legs from stg_fare_offers into offer-level microstructure (routing path, connecting hub, stop count, carriers, departure timing) and adds the derived trip axes (days_to_departure, stay_length) that constraint #3 forbids computing at ingestion. NOTE: an offer's legs describe the OUTBOUND journey only — Google Flights returns return options behind a departure_token we never call (constraint #1) — but offer_price is the full ROUND-TRIP total. Incremental by snapshot_date.

columns.description: Derived lead time: outbound_date - snapshot_date (DAY). The booking-curve x-axis. Derived from dates here, never trusted from the request's advance_days (a later assertion checks they agree).
Derived trip length: return_date - outbound_date (DAY).
Full ROUND-TRIP total price for this itinerary (USD). Attached to the outbound offer; there is no separate return price.
Outbound connections = n_legs - 1. ATL–CMN has no nonstop, so always >= 1.
Outbound airport path, e.g. 'ATL-YUL-CMN'.
First connecting airport on the outbound (NULL only if nonstop, which never happens on this route).
Local departure DATETIME of the first outbound leg (time-of-day microstructure).
Distinct operating carriers across the outbound legs.
Operating carrier of the first outbound leg.

  -- Reprocess the boundary partition (idempotent via MERGE + uniqueKey); scans
  -- only new/boundary partitions, never the whole table (constraint #2).

  *,
  EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) AS origin_departure_hour,
  -- NULL-safe: an unparseable departure time stays NULL rather than silently
  -- mislabeling as 'red_eye'.
  CASE
    WHEN origin_departure_time IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 5 AND 11 THEN 'morning'
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 12 AND 16 THEN 'afternoon'
    WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM origin_departure_time) BETWEEN 17 AND 21 THEN 'evening'
    ELSE 'red_eye'
  END AS departure_time_bucket
FROM offers

tables.query:
WITH offers AS (
  SELECT snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, days_to_departure,
         stay_length_days, offer_price
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_fare_offers`
  
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) AS days_to_departure,   -- constant across a trip's offers
  ANY_VALUE(stay_length_days) AS stay_length_days,
  CASE
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 14 THEN NULL
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 21 THEN 14
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 30 THEN 21
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 45 THEN 30
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 60 THEN 45
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 90 THEN 60
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 120 THEN 90
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 180 THEN 120
    ELSE 180
  END AS lead_bucket_min,
  EXTRACT(MONTH FROM outbound_date) AS departure_month,
  MIN(offer_price) AS cheapest_fare
FROM offers
GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date

actionDescriptor.description: The fare series: one row per trip-observation (grain: snapshot_date × outbound_date × return_date) with its CHEAPEST offer price and cell coordinates. The single definition of 'the fare for this trip at this observation' — int_daily_fare_stats aggregates it into sufficient statistics, and fct_fare_anomaly scores it, so the cheapest-per-trip + lead-bucket + month logic lives in exactly one place. Keeps ALL stay lengths (stay_length_days) so a future stay-length model can use the 14/21-day variants; the booking-curve/seasonal/anomaly layers filter to the default stay downstream. Carries outbound_date so cells can count DISTINCT ANCHORS (independent trips), which the re-observing panel makes different from a raw observation count. Incremental by snapshot_date.

tables.incrementalQuery:
WITH offers AS (
  SELECT snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, days_to_departure,
         stay_length_days, offer_price
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_fare_offers`
  WHERE snapshot_date >= (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`)
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) AS days_to_departure,   -- constant across a trip's offers
  ANY_VALUE(stay_length_days) AS stay_length_days,
  CASE
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 14 THEN NULL
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 21 THEN 14
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 30 THEN 21
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 45 THEN 30
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 60 THEN 45
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 90 THEN 60
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 120 THEN 90
    WHEN ANY_VALUE(days_to_departure) < 180 THEN 120
    ELSE 180
  END AS lead_bucket_min,
  EXTRACT(MONTH FROM outbound_date) AS departure_month,
  MIN(offer_price) AS cheapest_fare
FROM offers
GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date

tables.query:
WITH moments AS (
  SELECT
    departure_month,
    SUM(n_observations) AS n_observations,
    COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date) AS n_snapshots,
    SUM(sum_fare) AS sum_fare,
    SUM(sum_sq_fare) AS sum_sq_fare,
    MIN(min_fare) AS min_fare,
    MAX(max_fare) AS max_fare,
    SUM(sum_ln_fare) AS sum_ln_fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`
  GROUP BY departure_month
),

anchors AS (
  SELECT
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM outbound_date) AS departure_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT outbound_date) AS n_distinct_anchors
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL
    AND stay_length_days = 7
  GROUP BY departure_month
)

SELECT
  m.departure_month,
  CASE m.departure_month
    WHEN 1 THEN 'Jan' WHEN 2 THEN 'Feb' WHEN 3 THEN 'Mar' WHEN 4 THEN 'Apr'
    WHEN 5 THEN 'May' WHEN 6 THEN 'Jun' WHEN 7 THEN 'Jul' WHEN 8 THEN 'Aug'
    WHEN 9 THEN 'Sep' WHEN 10 THEN 'Oct' WHEN 11 THEN 'Nov' WHEN 12 THEN 'Dec'
  END AS month_name,
  m.n_observations,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) AS n_distinct_anchors,
  m.n_snapshots,
  SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations) AS mean_fare,
  IF(
    m.n_observations >= 2,
    SQRT(
      GREATEST(0.0, SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_sq_fare, m.n_observations)
                    - POW(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_fare, m.n_observations), 2))
      * m.n_observations / (m.n_observations - 1)
    ),
    NULL
  ) AS stddev_fare,
  EXP(SAFE_DIVIDE(m.sum_ln_fare, m.n_observations)) AS geomean_fare,
  ROUND(m.min_fare, 2) AS min_fare,
  ROUND(m.max_fare, 2) AS max_fare,
  COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) >= 5 AS is_reliable
FROM moments m
LEFT JOIN anchors a USING (departure_month)
ORDER BY m.departure_month

actionDescriptor.description: The seasonal component: expected CHEAPEST fare by DEPARTURE MONTH (grain: one row per departure_month, 1-12) for the DEFAULT-STAY product. Marginal over lead time — sums the additive sufficient stats of int_daily_fare_stats by month. Captures FIXED-CALENDAR seasonality (Morocco summer peak, school holidays); deliberately NOT the lunar Ramadan/Eid effect (drifts ~11 days/yr, so a month-of-year baseline smears it) — an event-relative baseline is the next step, keeping the fixed-vs-lunar seam explicit. MATURITY: is_reliable gates on DISTINCT ANCHORS, not n_observations. This marginal is for reporting/EDA and as a sparse-cell fallback — NOT the residual model's primary predictor (lead & month are collinear in the panel, so the additive formula is biased; the honest surface is the joint (lead × month) cell).

columns.description: Month of the departure date (1-12), pooled across years (month-of-year), so it reads as seasonality not a time series.
Human-readable month.
Trip-OBSERVATIONS pooled into this month (inflated by panel re-observation).
Distinct departure dates (independent trips) in this month. Drives is_reliable.
Expected (mean) cheapest fare for departures in this month.
Sample standard deviation (n-1). NULL when n_observations < 2.
n_distinct_anchors >= threshold.

tables.query:
WITH obs AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date,
    days_to_departure, lead_bucket_min, departure_month, stay_length_days,
    CAST(cheapest_fare AS FLOAT64) AS fare
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL
    AND stay_length_days = 7
),

-- Joint-cell sufficient stats (additive rollup) + the non-additive independent
-- sample count (distinct anchors) computed straight from the trip grain.
cell_moments AS (
  SELECT
    lead_bucket_min, departure_month,
    SUM(n_observations) AS cell_n,
    SUM(sum_fare) AS s, SUM(sum_sq_fare) AS q,
    SUM(sum_ln_fare) AS s_ln, SUM(sum_sq_ln_fare) AS q_ln
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min, departure_month
),
cell_anchors AS (
  SELECT
    lead_bucket_min, departure_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT outbound_date) AS n_distinct_anchors
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  WHERE lead_bucket_min IS NOT NULL
    AND stay_length_days = 7
  GROUP BY lead_bucket_min, departure_month
),

-- Leave-one-out moments from sufficient stats: remove this observation from its
-- cell so it can't inflate its own expectation. SAFE_DIVIDE throughout so the
-- low-N rows (which get gated out) yield NULL rather than a divide-by-zero error.
scored AS (
  SELECT
    o.*,
    m.cell_n,
    COALESCE(a.n_distinct_anchors, 0) AS n_distinct_anchors,
    -- raw-space LOO
    SAFE_DIVIDE(m.s - o.fare, m.cell_n - 1) AS loo_mean,
    SQRT(GREATEST(0.0, SAFE_DIVIDE(
      (m.q - POW(o.fare, 2)) - SAFE_DIVIDE(POW(m.s - o.fare, 2), m.cell_n - 1),
      m.cell_n - 2
    ))) AS loo_std,
    -- log-space LOO
    SAFE_DIVIDE(m.s_ln - LN(o.fare), m.cell_n - 1) AS loo_mean_ln,
    SQRT(GREATEST(0.0, SAFE_DIVIDE(
      (m.q_ln - POW(LN(o.fare), 2)) - SAFE_DIVIDE(POW(m.s_ln - LN(o.fare), 2), m.cell_n - 1),
      m.cell_n - 2
    ))) AS loo_std_ln
  FROM obs o
  JOIN cell_moments m USING (lead_bucket_min, departure_month)
  LEFT JOIN cell_anchors a USING (lead_bucket_min, departure_month)
),

flagged AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    -- a z is only meaningful with enough observations AND >1 independent anchor
    -- AND a non-degenerate spread
    (cell_n >= 3
     AND n_distinct_anchors >= 2
     AND loo_std IS NOT NULL AND loo_std > 0) AS can_score,
    (n_distinct_anchors >= 5) AS cell_is_reliable
  FROM scored
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  days_to_departure,
  lead_bucket_min,
  CASE lead_bucket_min
    WHEN 14 THEN '14-20'
    WHEN 21 THEN '21-29'
    WHEN 30 THEN '30-44'
    WHEN 45 THEN '45-59'
    WHEN 60 THEN '60-89'
    WHEN 90 THEN '90-119'
    WHEN 120 THEN '120-179'
    WHEN 180 THEN '180+'
  END AS lead_time_bucket,
  departure_month,
  stay_length_days,

  ROUND(fare, 2) AS observed_fare,
  ROUND(IF(can_score, loo_mean, NULL), 2) AS expected_fare,
  ROUND(IF(can_score, fare - loo_mean, NULL), 2) AS residual_usd,

  IF(can_score, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(fare - loo_mean, loo_std), 3), NULL) AS z_score,
  IF(can_score, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(LN(fare) - loo_mean_ln, loo_std_ln), 3), NULL) AS z_score_log,

  cell_n AS n_observations,
  n_distinct_anchors,
  cell_is_reliable,

  COALESCE(
    can_score
    AND cell_is_reliable
    AND ABS(SAFE_DIVIDE(fare - loo_mean, loo_std)) >= 2.5,
    FALSE
  ) AS is_anomaly,

  CASE
    WHEN can_score
         AND cell_is_reliable
         AND ABS(SAFE_DIVIDE(fare - loo_mean, loo_std)) >= 2.5
    THEN IF(fare > loo_mean, 'high', 'low')
  END AS anomaly_direction,

  CASE
    WHEN NOT can_score THEN 'insufficient_data'
    WHEN NOT cell_is_reliable THEN 'scored_unreliable'
    ELSE 'scored'
  END AS score_status
FROM flagged

actionDescriptor.description:
THE differentiator: the residual of each observed cheapest fare vs its fitted expectation, z-scored. Grain: one row per DEFAULT-STAY trip-observation (snapshot_date × outbound_date × return_date). Expected fare = the JOINT (lead_bucket × month) cell mean (conditions on lead AND season simultaneously, so it's free of the lead/month collinearity that biases an additive marginal surface), computed LEAVE-ONE-OUT from the cell's sufficient statistics so a point never scores against itself. z_score is raw-dollar (interpretable); z_score_log is the log-space robustness cross-check (fares are heavy-tailed).

Recomputed in full each run (type: table, not incremental): every observation re-scores against the current baseline, so early scores are never left stale as cells fill — correctness over an as-of incremental variant (a future operational 'would we have flagged it live' extension). Small (one thin route), and materialized for cheap consumer reads (constraint #5).

MATURITY: is_anomaly fires only when the cell is reliable by DISTINCT ANCHORS (independent trips) — not observation count, which the re-observing panel inflates. Today almost every cell is anchor-sparse, so this is honestly ~all 'insufficient_data'; it lights up as the panel accumulates. CAVEAT: within a cell, repeated re-observations of one anchor are autocorrelated, so loo_std under-states true dispersion (the anchor gate prevents unreliable cells from flagging but does not de-bias loo_std) — the fuller fix (one observation per anchor per cell) trades against the snapshot-partitioned idempotency and is deferred.

CONSUMER NOTE: because every re-observation is scored, one anchor's anomaly episode spans many rows — dedup to anchor-episodes before counting 'how many anomalies', or correlated rows multi-count.

tables.query:
SELECT
  -- Anomaly fact (grain: default-stay trip-observation)
  f.snapshot_date,
  f.outbound_date,
  f.return_date,
  f.days_to_departure,
  f.lead_time_bucket,
  f.departure_month,
  f.observed_fare,
  f.expected_fare,
  f.residual_usd,
  f.z_score,
  f.z_score_log,
  f.n_observations,
  f.n_distinct_anchors,
  f.cell_is_reliable,
  f.is_anomaly,
  f.anomaly_direction,
  f.score_status,

  -- Event context of the departure (from int_event_context, keyed on outbound_date)
  e.in_ramadan,
  e.in_summer_school_holiday,
  e.days_to_nearest_eid,
  e.nearest_eid_category,
  e.nearest_eid_date,
  e.eid_proximity,
  e.days_to_nearest_point_event,
  e.nearest_point_event_category,
  e.nearest_point_event_is_peak
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_marts.fct_fare_anomaly` f
LEFT JOIN `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_event_context` e USING (outbound_date)

actionDescriptor.description:
THE event-correlation surface (docs/warehouse.md layer #5, the marquee lunar-drift wrinkle): fct_fare_anomaly enriched with each departure's Moroccan-event context, so a consumer can ask 'do the fare residuals spike near Ramadan/Eid?' — the whole reason the DAG is multi-source. A thin VIEW joining the centerpiece fact to int_event_context on outbound_date; adds no modeling, changes no z-score.

WHY A CORRELATION VIEW, NOT AN EVENT PREDICTOR: the honest expected surface stays the joint (lead × month) cell mean (fct_fare_anomaly). Folding event proximity INTO the expectation would need many independent anchors spanning each lunar phase — which accumulate over a year (maturity discipline, docs/decisions.md). Until then this view lets the event effect be OBSERVED and charted against residuals without over-fitting a sparse predictor.

MATURITY TODAY: fct_fare_anomaly is still ~all 'insufficient_data' (cells anchor-sparse), so is_anomaly is mostly FALSE and the event correlation is not yet statistically testable — but the plumbing is live and one anchor (2027-02-15) already sits inside Ramadan 2027, so the surface fills as the panel rolls forward. CONSUMER NOTE (inherited): every re-observation is a row and one anchor's episode spans many rows — dedup to anchor-episodes before counting anomalies-near-events.

columns.description: Residual z vs the leave-one-out joint-cell expectation (NULL until the cell can score). See fct_fare_anomaly.
Reliable cell AND |z| >= threshold. Correlate the TRUE rows against the event columns.
Departure inside a Ramadan window.
Signed days departure→nearest Eid; the marquee demand-proximity axis to plot residuals against.
Coarse Eid travel zone (arrive_for_eid / pre_eid_month / post_eid / off_peak).

tables.query:
-- Raw curated occurrences: (id, category, start, end). end = start for point events.
-- Derived attributes (name / shape / is_lunar / is_travel_peak) are computed below
-- from the category so the literal rows stay compact and can't disagree with the
-- classification.
WITH events AS (
  SELECT * FROM UNNEST([
    -- Ramadan (holy month, window). Lower travel DURING (fasting at home); the
    -- END triggers the Eid al-Fitr family-visit peak. Drifts ~11 days/yr earlier.
    STRUCT('ramadan_2026' AS event_id, 'ramadan' AS event_category, DATE '2026-02-17' AS start_date, DATE '2026-03-19' AS end_date),
    STRUCT('ramadan_2027', 'ramadan', DATE '2027-02-08', DATE '2027-03-09'),
    STRUCT('ramadan_2028', 'ramadan', DATE '2028-01-28', DATE '2028-02-25'),

    -- Eid al-Fitr (end of Ramadan) — a marquee diaspora travel peak (point).
    STRUCT('eid_al_fitr_2026', 'eid_al_fitr', DATE '2026-03-20', DATE '2026-03-20'),
    STRUCT('eid_al_fitr_2027', 'eid_al_fitr', DATE '2027-03-10', DATE '2027-03-10'),
    STRUCT('eid_al_fitr_2028', 'eid_al_fitr', DATE '2028-02-26', DATE '2028-02-26'),

    -- Eid al-Adha (Feast of Sacrifice) — the other major family-visit peak (point).
    STRUCT('eid_al_adha_2026', 'eid_al_adha', DATE '2026-05-27', DATE '2026-05-27'),
    STRUCT('eid_al_adha_2027', 'eid_al_adha', DATE '2027-05-17', DATE '2027-05-17'),
    STRUCT('eid_al_adha_2028', 'eid_al_adha', DATE '2028-05-05', DATE '2028-05-05'),

    -- Mawlid (Prophet's Birthday) — secondary lunar marker (point).
    STRUCT('mawlid_2026', 'mawlid', DATE '2026-08-25', DATE '2026-08-25'),
    STRUCT('mawlid_2027', 'mawlid', DATE '2027-08-15', DATE '2027-08-15'),
    STRUCT('mawlid_2028', 'mawlid', DATE '2028-08-04', DATE '2028-08-04'),

    -- Hijra (Islamic) New Year — minor lunar marker, anchors the drift story (point).
    STRUCT('hijra_new_year_2026', 'hijra_new_year', DATE '2026-06-17', DATE '2026-06-17'),
    STRUCT('hijra_new_year_2027', 'hijra_new_year', DATE '2027-06-06', DATE '2027-06-06'),
    STRUCT('hijra_new_year_2028', 'hijra_new_year', DATE '2028-05-26', DATE '2028-05-26'),

    -- Summer school holiday (fixed-calendar leisure peak, window; approximate span).
    STRUCT('summer_school_holiday_2026', 'summer_school_holiday', DATE '2026-07-05', DATE '2026-09-05'),
    STRUCT('summer_school_holiday_2027', 'summer_school_holiday', DATE '2027-07-05', DATE '2027-09-05'),
    STRUCT('summer_school_holiday_2028', 'summer_school_holiday', DATE '2028-07-05', DATE '2028-09-05')
  ])
)

SELECT
  event_id,
  event_category,
  CASE event_category
    WHEN 'ramadan' THEN 'Ramadan'
    WHEN 'eid_al_fitr' THEN 'Eid al-Fitr'
    WHEN 'eid_al_adha' THEN 'Eid al-Adha'
    WHEN 'mawlid' THEN "Mawlid (Prophet's Birthday)"
    WHEN 'hijra_new_year' THEN 'Hijra New Year'
    WHEN 'summer_school_holiday' THEN 'Summer school holiday'
  END AS event_name,
  start_date,
  end_date,
  IF(start_date = end_date, 'point', 'window') AS event_shape,
  DATE_DIFF(end_date, start_date, DAY) + 1 AS duration_days,
  -- Lunar events drift ~11 days/yr against the Gregorian calendar — the reason a
  -- month-of-year seasonal baseline can't see them and an event-relative one can.
  event_category IN ('ramadan', 'eid_al_fitr', 'eid_al_adha', 'mawlid', 'hijra_new_year') AS is_lunar,
  -- The high-demand drivers for this route (Eid family visits + summer leisure),
  -- vs the minor lunar markers — lets the correlation view focus on peaks.
  event_category IN ('eid_al_fitr', 'eid_al_adha', 'summer_school_holiday') AS is_travel_peak,
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM start_date) AS calendar_year
FROM events

actionDescriptor.description:
Curated Moroccan travel-demand event calendar — the multi-source seam that makes the seasonal/anomaly layers event-aware (docs/warehouse.md layer #5). Grain: one row per event OCCURRENCE (a specific year's Ramadan, Eid, etc.). Deliberately scoped to the demand drivers of a US→Morocco diaspora leisure route: the LUNAR religious events (Ramadan window + the Eid family-visit peaks + Mawlid/Hijra markers) that DRIFT ~11 days/yr against the Gregorian calendar — the marquee wrinkle a month-of-year seasonal baseline structurally cannot capture — plus the FIXED summer school-holiday window (the big leisure peak already visible in the fares). Single-day civic holidays (Throne Day, Green March, Independence Day) are intentionally excluded: low leisure-travel signal for this route, and they'd add breadth over depth.

SHAPE: point events (Eid, Mawlid, Hijra New Year) get start_date = end_date and are consumed as SIGNED days-to-event; windows (Ramadan, summer school) get a real span and are consumed as in_window flags — never conflate a month-long fast with a one-day feast (int_event_context keeps them distinct).

PROVENANCE: dates curated from timeanddate.com / publicholidays.africa / holidays-info.com (Morocco). LUNAR dates depend on moon sighting and may differ from the observed Moroccan date by ±1 day; the summer school-holiday window is APPROXIMATE (public-school break, ~early Jul–early Sep). Static data-as-code (user decision 2026-07-12) — deterministic, no API budget, reproducible; lunar dates are publishable years ahead. Refresh the far-out years as observed dates firm up.

tables.query:
WITH valid_snapshots AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date,
    captured_at,
    origin,
    destination,
    outbound_date,
    return_date,
    currency,
    payload
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots`
  WHERE is_valid_fetch
  -- `>=` reprocesses the boundary partition (idempotent via MERGE + uniqueKey),
  -- so late/retried snapshots that reached staging after a same-day run still flow through.
  
),

offers AS (
  SELECT
    s.snapshot_date,
    s.captured_at,
    s.origin,
    s.destination,
    s.outbound_date,
    s.return_date,
    s.currency,
    src.offer_list,
    offer_index,
    offer,
    ARRAY_LENGTH(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(offer, '$.flights')) AS n_legs
  FROM valid_snapshots AS s,
  -- Union best_flights + other_flights, tagging which list each came from.
  UNNEST([
    STRUCT('best'  AS offer_list, JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(s.payload, '$.best_flights')  AS arr),
    STRUCT('other' AS offer_list, JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(s.payload, '$.other_flights') AS arr)
  ]) AS src,
  UNNEST(src.arr) AS offer WITH OFFSET AS offer_index
)

SELECT
  o.snapshot_date,
  o.captured_at,
  o.origin,
  o.destination,
  o.outbound_date,
  o.return_date,
  o.currency,
  o.offer_list,
  o.offer_index,
  o.n_legs,
  leg_index,

  -- Offer-level (repeated per leg — the offer is the round-trip itinerary).
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.price') AS INT64) AS offer_price,
  JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.type') AS offer_type,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.total_duration') AS INT64) AS total_duration_min,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.carbon_emissions.this_flight') AS INT64) AS carbon_emissions_g,
  ARRAY_LENGTH(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(o.offer, '$.layovers')) AS n_layovers,
  JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.departure_token') AS departure_token,

  -- Leg-level.
  UPPER(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.id')) AS leg_departure_airport,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.name') AS leg_departure_airport_name,
  SAFE.PARSE_DATETIME('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M', JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.time')) AS leg_departure_time,
  UPPER(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.id')) AS leg_arrival_airport,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.name') AS leg_arrival_airport_name,
  SAFE.PARSE_DATETIME('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M', JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.time')) AS leg_arrival_time,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.duration') AS INT64) AS leg_duration_min,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.airline') AS leg_airline,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.flight_number') AS leg_flight_number,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.airplane') AS leg_airplane,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.travel_class') AS leg_travel_class,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.legroom') AS leg_legroom
FROM offers AS o,
UNNEST(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(o.offer, '$.flights')) AS leg WITH OFFSET AS leg_index

actionDescriptor.description: Tidy offer×leg rows — the deeply-nested raw_response flattened (grain: snapshot × offer × leg). best_flights and other_flights are unioned with an offer_list label; each offer's flights array is unnested to legs. STRUCTURAL ONLY: unnest, cast, standardize codes/timestamps. Analytical derivations (days_to_departure, stop count, routing/hub, dispersion) belong in intermediate, per constraint #3. Reads only valid fetches from stg_fare_snapshots. Incremental by snapshot_date.

tables.incrementalQuery:
WITH valid_snapshots AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date,
    captured_at,
    origin,
    destination,
    outbound_date,
    return_date,
    currency,
    payload
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots`
  WHERE is_valid_fetch
  -- `>=` reprocesses the boundary partition (idempotent via MERGE + uniqueKey),
  -- so late/retried snapshots that reached staging after a same-day run still flow through.
  AND snapshot_date >= (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_offers`)
),

offers AS (
  SELECT
    s.snapshot_date,
    s.captured_at,
    s.origin,
    s.destination,
    s.outbound_date,
    s.return_date,
    s.currency,
    src.offer_list,
    offer_index,
    offer,
    ARRAY_LENGTH(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(offer, '$.flights')) AS n_legs
  FROM valid_snapshots AS s,
  -- Union best_flights + other_flights, tagging which list each came from.
  UNNEST([
    STRUCT('best'  AS offer_list, JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(s.payload, '$.best_flights')  AS arr),
    STRUCT('other' AS offer_list, JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(s.payload, '$.other_flights') AS arr)
  ]) AS src,
  UNNEST(src.arr) AS offer WITH OFFSET AS offer_index
)

SELECT
  o.snapshot_date,
  o.captured_at,
  o.origin,
  o.destination,
  o.outbound_date,
  o.return_date,
  o.currency,
  o.offer_list,
  o.offer_index,
  o.n_legs,
  leg_index,

  -- Offer-level (repeated per leg — the offer is the round-trip itinerary).
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.price') AS INT64) AS offer_price,
  JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.type') AS offer_type,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.total_duration') AS INT64) AS total_duration_min,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.carbon_emissions.this_flight') AS INT64) AS carbon_emissions_g,
  ARRAY_LENGTH(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(o.offer, '$.layovers')) AS n_layovers,
  JSON_VALUE(o.offer, '$.departure_token') AS departure_token,

  -- Leg-level.
  UPPER(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.id')) AS leg_departure_airport,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.name') AS leg_departure_airport_name,
  SAFE.PARSE_DATETIME('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M', JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.departure_airport.time')) AS leg_departure_time,
  UPPER(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.id')) AS leg_arrival_airport,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.name') AS leg_arrival_airport_name,
  SAFE.PARSE_DATETIME('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M', JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.arrival_airport.time')) AS leg_arrival_time,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.duration') AS INT64) AS leg_duration_min,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.airline') AS leg_airline,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.flight_number') AS leg_flight_number,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.airplane') AS leg_airplane,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.travel_class') AS leg_travel_class,
  JSON_VALUE(leg, '$.legroom') AS leg_legroom
FROM offers AS o,
UNNEST(JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(o.offer, '$.flights')) AS leg WITH OFFSET AS leg_index

tables.query:
WITH scoped AS (
  SELECT
    captured_at,
    snapshot_date,
    origin,
    destination,
    outbound_date,
    return_date,
    advance_days,
    return_gap_days,
    currency,
    http_status,
    raw_response,
    -- Parse once. SAFE.PARSE_JSON returns NULL for the 'REQUEST_ERROR:' bodies
    -- (transport failures) and any malformed JSON, so it doubles as a validity check.
    SAFE.PARSE_JSON(raw_response) AS payload
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_raw.fare_snapshots`
  WHERE origin = 'ATL'
    AND destination = 'CMN'
  -- `>=` (not `>`) reprocesses the boundary partition so a same-day re-run picks
  -- up late/retried arrivals into today's partition; the MERGE + QUALIFY below
  -- make that idempotent. Still scans one partition, never the whole raw table.
  
),

deduped AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM scoped
  -- Collapse duplicate / retried snapshots of the same trip within a run.
  -- Latest capture wins; raw_response breaks captured_at ties deterministically.
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    PARTITION BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date
    ORDER BY captured_at DESC, raw_response DESC
  ) = 1
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  captured_at,
  origin,
  destination,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  advance_days,      -- request metadata; days_to_departure is derived downstream
  return_gap_days,   -- request metadata; stay_length is derived downstream
  currency,
  http_status,
  (http_status = 200 AND payload IS NOT NULL) AS is_valid_fetch,
  payload,
  -- price_insights: Google's own per-call comparison signal (free external
  -- baseline, never our centerpiece). NULL on failed fetches.
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.lowest_price') AS INT64) AS gf_lowest_price,
  JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.price_level') AS gf_price_level,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.typical_price_range[0]') AS INT64) AS gf_typical_price_low,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.typical_price_range[1]') AS INT64) AS gf_typical_price_high
FROM deduped

actionDescriptor.description: One row per DEDUPED, route-scoped fare snapshot (grain: snapshot_date × outbound_date × return_date). Dedup happens HERE, before any offer×leg fan-out, so duplicate/retried runs collapse first. Keeps every scoped row (incl. failed fetches) with an is_valid_fetch flag so the freshness assertion can tell 'run never fired' from 'run fired, fetch failed'. Parses raw_response once into a JSON column for downstream unnest; lifts the per-call price_insights (Google's own comparison signal). Incremental: scans only new snapshot_date partitions — dedup is fully within-partition because a run always writes its own ingestion-day partition.

tables.incrementalQuery:
WITH scoped AS (
  SELECT
    captured_at,
    snapshot_date,
    origin,
    destination,
    outbound_date,
    return_date,
    advance_days,
    return_gap_days,
    currency,
    http_status,
    raw_response,
    -- Parse once. SAFE.PARSE_JSON returns NULL for the 'REQUEST_ERROR:' bodies
    -- (transport failures) and any malformed JSON, so it doubles as a validity check.
    SAFE.PARSE_JSON(raw_response) AS payload
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_raw.fare_snapshots`
  WHERE origin = 'ATL'
    AND destination = 'CMN'
  -- `>=` (not `>`) reprocesses the boundary partition so a same-day re-run picks
  -- up late/retried arrivals into today's partition; the MERGE + QUALIFY below
  -- make that idempotent. Still scans one partition, never the whole raw table.
  AND snapshot_date >= (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots`)
),

deduped AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM scoped
  -- Collapse duplicate / retried snapshots of the same trip within a run.
  -- Latest capture wins; raw_response breaks captured_at ties deterministically.
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    PARTITION BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date
    ORDER BY captured_at DESC, raw_response DESC
  ) = 1
)

SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  captured_at,
  origin,
  destination,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  advance_days,      -- request metadata; days_to_departure is derived downstream
  return_gap_days,   -- request metadata; stay_length is derived downstream
  currency,
  http_status,
  (http_status = 200 AND payload IS NOT NULL) AS is_valid_fetch,
  payload,
  -- price_insights: Google's own per-call comparison signal (free external
  -- baseline, never our centerpiece). NULL on failed fetches.
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.lowest_price') AS INT64) AS gf_lowest_price,
  JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.price_level') AS gf_price_level,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.typical_price_range[0]') AS INT64) AS gf_typical_price_low,
  SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.price_insights.typical_price_range[1]') AS INT64) AS gf_typical_price_high
FROM deduped

assertions.query:
SELECT
  o.snapshot_date,
  o.outbound_date,
  o.return_date,
  o.days_to_departure,
  s.advance_days,
  o.stay_length_days,
  s.return_gap_days
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_fare_offers` o
JOIN `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots` s
  USING (snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date)
WHERE o.days_to_departure != s.advance_days
   OR o.stay_length_days != s.return_gap_days

actionDescriptor.description: INTEGRITY: the trip axes DERIVED downstream from dates (constraint #3 — never trust the request) must agree with the request metadata ingestion recorded. days_to_departure (= outbound − snapshot) must equal advance_days, and stay_length_days (= return − outbound) must equal return_gap_days. A mismatch means an ingestion date-boundary/timezone bug (the run's snapshot_date disagreeing with the advance_days it computed at request time) — exactly the class of silent corruption the derive-downstream discipline exists to surface. Joins the offer grain back to the deduped snapshot on the trip key.

assertions.query:
SELECT
  snapshot_date,
  outbound_date,
  return_date,
  offer_list,
  offer_index,
  offer_price
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_fare_offers`
WHERE offer_price IS NULL
   OR offer_price < 50
   OR offer_price > 20000

actionDescriptor.description: REASONABLENESS bounds on the priced offer — catches parse/unnest corruption before it poisons the baselines (a single $0 or $1e6 fare would wreck a cell mean and z-score). Deliberately WIDE, not a business rule: observed economy round-trips run ~$900–$3200 (premium connecting routings), so bounds of $50–$20000 flag only garbage (negative, zero, NULL, or absurd), never a legitimately expensive itinerary. Fires per offending offer so the bad row is identifiable.

assertions.query:
-- Cadence is `0 9 */2 * *` (every 2 days), and Scheduler retries a transient
-- failure the same day, so a healthy pipeline always has a valid fetch within
-- ~2 days. >3 days stale means a full cycle (fire + all retries) was missed —
-- the alarm point. Returns a row ONLY when stale, which fails the assertion.
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) AS last_valid_snapshot
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots`
  WHERE is_valid_fetch
)
SELECT
  last_valid_snapshot,
  DATE_DIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), last_valid_snapshot, DAY) AS days_since_valid_fetch
FROM latest
WHERE last_valid_snapshot IS NULL
   OR DATE_DIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), last_valid_snapshot, DAY) > 3

actionDescriptor.description: FRESHNESS backstop — the continuous-accumulation guarantee (constraint #4). Fails when no VALID fetch has landed recently, i.e. the ingestion pipeline has silently stopped writing. Uses is_valid_fetch (not just any row) so it distinguishes 'run never fired' from 'run fired but every fetch failed' — a partition full of error rows is still stale for modeling. Complements the per-call error isolation + Scheduler retries: those keep one run resilient; this detects a run that stopped happening at all.

assertions.query:
SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, lead_bucket_min, departure_month,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_daily_fare_stats`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, lead_bucket_min, departure_month
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, offer_list, offer_index,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_fare_offers`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, offer_list, offer_index
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_intermediate.int_trip_fares`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_marts.fct_fare_anomaly`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    event_id,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
  GROUP BY event_id
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  'end_date >= start_date' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (end_date >= start_date)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'start_date >= DATE \'2025-01-01\' AND start_date < DATE \'2029-01-01\'' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (start_date >= DATE '2025-01-01' AND start_date < DATE '2029-01-01')
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'event_id IS NOT NULL' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (event_id IS NOT NULL)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'event_category IS NOT NULL' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (event_category IS NOT NULL)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'start_date IS NOT NULL' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (start_date IS NOT NULL)
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'end_date IS NOT NULL' AS failing_row_condition,
  *
FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_reference.dim_events`
WHERE NOT (end_date IS NOT NULL)

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, offer_list, offer_index, leg_index,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_offers`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date, offer_list, offer_index, leg_index
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

SELECT
  *
FROM (
  SELECT
    snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date,
    COUNT(1) AS index_row_count
  FROM `airfare-drift.airfare_staging.stg_fare_snapshots`
  GROUP BY snapshot_date, outbound_date, return_date
  ) AS data
WHERE index_row_count > 1

actionDescriptor.description: Raw, append-only SerpApi Google Flights responses for the ATL–CMN panel. One row per panel query per run; `raw_response` holds the full API body verbatim (STRING). Deliberately messy: duplicate/late snapshots and the deeply-nested offer arrays are preserved here — all parsing/dedup happens downstream in staging (constraint #3). Partitioned by `snapshot_date`, clustered by (origin, destination).

columns.description: Per-row wall-clock time the fetch completed (UTC TIMESTAMP). Used to pick the latest of duplicate snapshots during dedup.
Ingestion-run date (DATE), fixed at run start so all of a run's rows share one partition. The observation date for the booking curve.
Requested departure airport IATA code (always ATL post-pivot).
Requested arrival airport IATA code (always CMN post-pivot).
Requested departure date (DATE) — a panel anchor.
Requested return date (DATE).
Request-time lead: outbound_date - snapshot_date. REQUEST METADATA — downstream derives its own days_to_departure and asserts they agree.
Request-time stay length: return_date - outbound_date. Request metadata.
Requested currency (always USD).
SerpApi HTTP status. 200 = success; non-200 = captured error body; NULL = transport-level failure (raw_response starts with 'REQUEST_ERROR:'). Staging treats non-200/NULL as a failed fetch.
Full API response body as-is (STRING). JSON on success; a 'REQUEST_ERROR: ...' string on transport failure.
